code:
from django.http import HttpResponse

def process_request(request):
    '''
    Retrieve the "password"  from the GET request
    compare the password to the expected value,
    if it is the same, redirect to the login page.
    '''
    password = request.GET["password"]
    if password == "myPa55word":
        redirect("login")

label: vulnerable
description: GHSA-cmc7-mfmr-xqrx: Logic error in authentication in proxy.py
fix: # Upgrade proxy.py to a patched version.  See GHSA-cmc7-mfmr-xqrx.
attack_scenario: before_upstream_connection in AuthPlugin in http/proxy/auth.py in proxy.py before 2.3.1 accepts incorrect Proxy-Authorization header data because of a boolean confusion (and versus or).

label: vulnerable
description: GHSA-j2pw-vp55-fqqj: Flask-AppBuilder vulnerable to incorrect authentication when using auth type OpenID
fix: # Upgrade Flask-AppBuilder to a patched version.  See GHSA-j2pw-vp55-fqqj.
attack_scenario:
### Impact
When Flask-AppBuilder is set to AUTH_TYPE AUTH_OID, allows an attacker to forge an HTTP request, that could deceive the backend into using any requested OpenID service. This vulnerability could grant an attacker unauthorised privilege access if a custom OpenID service is deployed by the a

label: vulnerable
description: GHSA-q485-cg9q-xq2r: Improper Authentication and Origin Validation Error in pyload-ng
fix: # Upgrade pyload-ng to a patched version.  See GHSA-q485-cg9q-xq2r.
attack_scenario:
### Summary

A Host Header Spoofing vulnerability in the `@local_check` decorator allows unauthenticated external attackers to bypass local-only restrictions. This grants access to the Click'N'Load API endpoints, enabling attackers to remotely queue arbitrary downloads, leading to Server-Side Reques

label: vulnerable
code:
bash
docker run -d --name nltk-wordnet-web-default-retest -p 8004:8004 \
  nltk-sandbox \
  python -c "import nltk; nltk.download('wordnet', quiet=True); from nltk.app.wordnet_app import wnb; wnb(8004, True)"
description: GHSA-jm6w-m3j8-898g: Unauthenticated remote shutdown in nltk.app.wordnet_app
fix: # Upgrade nltk to a patched version.  See GHSA-jm6w-m3j8-898g.
attack_scenario:
### Summary
`nltk.app.wordnet_app` allows unauthenticated remote shutdown of the local WordNet Browser HTTP server when it is started in its default mode. A simple `GET /SHUTDOWN%20THE%20SERVER` request causes the process to terminate immediately via `os._exit(0)`, resulting in a denial of service.

label: vulnerable
code:
@router.post("/build_public_tmp/{flow_id}/flow")
async def build_public_tmp(
    *,
    flow_id: uuid.UUID,
    data: Annotated[FlowDataRequest | None, Body(embed=True)] = None,  # ATTACKER CONTROLLED
    request: Request,
    # ... NO Depends(get_current_active_user) -- MISSING AUTH ...
):
    """Build a public flow without requiring authentication."""
    client_id = request.cookies.get("client_id")
    owner_user, new_flow_id = await verify_public_flow_and_get_user(flow_id=flow_id, client_id=client_id)

    job_id = await start_flow_build(
        flow_id=new_flow_id,
        data=data,  # Attacker's data passed directly to graph builder
        current_user=owner_user,
        ...
    )
description: GHSA-vwmf-pq79-vjvx: Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution in Langflow via Public Flow Build Endpoint
fix: # Upgrade langflow to a patched version.  See GHSA-vwmf-pq79-vjvx.
attack_scenario:
## Summary

The `POST /api/v1/build_public_tmp/{flow_id}/flow` endpoint allows building public flows without requiring authentication. When the optional `data` parameter is supplied, the endpoint uses **attacker-controlled flow data** (containing arbitrary Python code in node definitions) instead of
